feedback: Add to the explainer and resubmit. The sim's core idea — antenna length sets the detectable frequency — is solid. To understand why L ≈ λ/2 works, think about what happens to current in the antenna: the tips are open ends, so current must be zero there. That forces a standing wave boundary condition, exactly like a string fixed at both ends. The shortest standing wave that fits zero-current nodes at both tips spans exactly one half-cycle — so L = λ/2. At that length the antenna's impedance is purely resistive and energy transfer is maximized; off resonance there's a reactive component that fights it.
Limiting case at β=0 shows γ=0 instead of 1 — fix the denominator in the sim.
